text.title: growth-community-building Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for growth-community-building as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Over-moderating: removing posts aggressively or requiring approval before posts are visible.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Heavy moderation kills spontaneity and signals distrust. Members self-censor when they feel monitored. Light touch moderation with clear guidelines produces better outcomes.

failure-mode.description: Only the founder posting — treating the community as a broadcast channel.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If members can only receive content rather than contribute it, the community is a newsletter. Communities require member-generated content to produce the connection value that justifies membership.

failure-mode.description: Growing headcount faster than community health metrics support.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Rapid growth in a community with low DAU/MAU dilutes conversation quality and makes new members feel like they joined after the good times. Quality of engagement must precede scale of membership.
